new_classes.label_en: Wine with PDO in containers > 2 L with actual alcoholic strength ≤ 13% vol (CN 2204 29 38)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.U. in Behältern > 2 L mit einem vorhandenen Alkoholgehalt von ≤ 13% vol (KN 2204 29 38)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected designation of origin (PDO), presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength not exceeding 13% vol, classified under CN code 2204 29 38.

coverage_explanation: The three key distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 29 38 are captured: (1) PDO status via a someValuesFrom restriction on PDOOriginQuality, (2) container volume > 2 L via someValuesFrom on LargeContainer (which is already defined in the TBox as Packaging with Volume ≥ 2 L), and (3) actual alcoholic strength ≤ 13% vol via a decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent. The legal text (additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22) that defines precisely what constitutes a PDO wine is cross-referenced but not reproduced here, so the formal content of that note (specific PDO definitions, labelling conditions, etc.) cannot be axiomatised without access to the full text of additional note 6(a) — this is the primary gap reducing the score from 1.0. The lower bound of the alcoholic strength range (i.e. the minimum, implied to be above the semi-sparkling/other threshold) is also not explicitly encoded, as the legal text fragment does not specify it for this subheading.
